data.title: Foreign Ministry responds to Trump's claim to prevent China from getting involved in the cryptocurrency field
data.description: Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun hosted a regular press conference. A foreign journalist asked about US President Trump's statement in Davos that he is committed to maintaining the US's leading position in the cryptocurrency field and that measures must be taken to prevent China from gaining a foothold in this area. What is China's comment on this? Guo Jiakun stated that China has always actively engaged in multilateral and bilateral monetary and financial cooperation, participated in global macroeconomic policy coordination and the formulation of international financial rules. "Regarding the specific questions you mentioned, I suggest you inquire with the relevant Chinese authorities." (Beijing Daily)

data.description: GMGN data shows that RALPH briefly plummeted by 67%, with its market capitalization now at $12.31 million. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should exercise caution.

data.title: CZ: Optimistic about the future in three major areas: tokenization, payments, and AI.
data.description: Speaking at the "A New Era of Finance" panel discussion at the World Economic Forum in Davos, CZ stated, "The overall scale of trading platforms has also surpassed that of last year. Currently, the crypto industry has two mature sectors: trading platforms and stablecoins. Looking ahead, I am optimistic about three new directions: First, tokenization is a very important direction. By tokenizing some assets, governments can actually solve financial problems more efficiently, improve the operational efficiency of the financial system, and thereby promote the development of related industries and trading markets. Second is payments. We have tried crypto payments in the past, but frankly, not many people actually use them. However, a trend is emerging: traditional payment methods are merging with crypto technology. For example, users use Visa, Mastercard, and other cards to complete payments, funds are deducted from their accounts, merchants receive fiat currency, and settlement and bridging are handled through stablecoins and blockchain. This model is gradually being implemented and will definitely develop in the future. The third direction is artificial intelligence (AI). He believes that the 'native currency' of AI agents should naturally be cryptocurrency, and blockchain is currently the most suitable native technology interface for AI agents. Today's AI is not yet truly..." Agents won't buy you plane tickets, book restaurants, or complete payments directly; but once AI truly has the ability to act and transact, cryptocurrency will become its most natural and native payment and settlement method.

data.title: CZ: Predicts a significant decrease in demand from physical banks over the next 10 years.
data.description: At the World Economic Forum in Davos, CZ stated that demand for physical banks will decrease significantly over the next 10 years. With the development of technologies such as blockchain and KYC, the need for users to visit physical banks is declining. While banks won't disappear, their branch networks will shrink considerably. Furthermore, he expressed skepticism about the direct use of Bitcoin for payments, citing the extremely high failure rate in this area despite years of industry investment. Zhao Changpeng believes that Meme tokens, similar to previous NFTs and Metaverse, are extremely risky and highly speculative. While projects with cultural value like Dogecoin may exist for a long time, most Meme tokens will not be sustainable.

data.title: Gate DEX BountyDrop launches airdrop events for Anichess and Neura.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Gate DEX's BountyDrop has launched the Anichess and Neura airdrop events. Users can participate in the tasks through the BountyDrop module on the Gate DEX "Events" page. 2,000 participants who complete the Anichess task will be randomly selected to share $10,000 CHECK; 1,000 participants who complete the Neura task will be randomly selected to share 10,000 USDT. In addition, Gate DEX is running the "SOL Chain Newcomer Carnival" event from January 19th to February 5th. During the event, new Gate Wallet SOL Chain Swap users who complete their first transaction will receive a gas fee subsidy. Furthermore, users who invite friends to participate will receive a 30% transaction rebate, and invited friends will receive a 20% transaction rebate. Gate DEX provides a one-stop connection for 100+ public chains, millions of assets, and tens of thousands of DApps, offering optimal solutions for every Web3 need. BountyDrop is a dedicated earning activity section on the Gate DEX discovery page, where users can complete simple tasks for a chance to win rewards or participate in a lottery for airdropped tokens.

data.title: Analysts: Amid heightened macroeconomic uncertainty, institutional investors are tightening their risk exposure.
data.description: The US spot Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of $708.7 million yesterday, the largest single-day outflow in nearly two months, while the Ethereum ETF saw a net outflow of $286.9 million. Amidst heightened macroeconomic uncertainty, institutional investors are further reducing their risk exposure. Rachael Lucas, a crypto analyst at BTC Markets, stated that Wednesday's outflows were more of a typical "risk-off" behavior. She pointed out that when the macroeconomic environment turns unfavorable, such as rising interest rates, escalating geopolitical risks, or a sudden increase in market volatility, institutional investors tend to be the first to withdraw funds from high-beta assets. This is not a signal of structural weakness, but rather institutions tightening their risk exposure before uncertainty sets in, and does not mean abandoning the crypto asset class altogether. (The Block)

data.title: ING: Inflation may reshape the Bank of Japan's interest rate hike plans
data.description: Japan's CPI data, due ahead of Friday's interest rate decision, is expected to show a significant drop in December's inflation rate. Analysts at ING Bank stated that a decline in inflation could prompt the Bank of Japan to reassess its future interest rate hike plans. "Strong wage growth and government aid measures will keep core inflation above 2%. Once the Bank of Japan confirms that core inflation will consistently remain above 2% and higher than overall inflation, it is likely to take further action sometime in the second half of 2026." (Jinshi)
